titleStmt.title: SII 17.541: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 541. (A.R. No. 499 of 1904.) VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the north wall of the first prākāra in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.

This records the sale (āḷvilai-pramāṇa-iśaivu-tīṭṭu) of 10 slaves with their associates of the Śadaiyāṇḍi-kūṭṭam for 1,000 kāśu by Ariyāṉ Pichchaṉ alias Ediriliśōḻa-Gaṅgaināḍāḻvān, the kāval officer of Mēṟpuram in Kuṉṟūr-nāḍu to the authorities of the temple of Tirumaṟaikkāḍu-uḍaiyār. Five of these ten slaves are stated to have been once sold to the god by Mudaliyār Gaṅgappiḷḷai alias Rājarāja-Gaṅgaināḍāḻvār.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarā[ja*]tevaṟku yāṇṭu 3vatu Umpaḷaṉāṭṭuk kuṉṟūrnāṭṭu Uṭaiyār tirumaṟaikkāṭuṭaiyār koyil
Ātitaṇṭecura tevarkaṉmikaḷukku Innāṭṭu meṟpuram kāval Ariyāṉ piccaṉāṉa Etirilicoḻa kaṅkaināṭāḻvāṉeṉ Āḷvilaippiramā
ṇa Icaivu tiṭṭuk kuṭutta paricāvatu Eṉ Aṭiyāril cataiyāṇṭi kūṭṭattu vetaṉāṉa paṭṭa nāyaka kaṇṇappaṉaiyum kurampaṉ Ātināyaka
kkaṇṇappaṉaiyum Uṭaiyāṉāṉa cokkanāyakak kaṇṇappaṉaiyum kaṅkaṉaiyum tiruvaṉaiyum muṉṉāḷile Eṅkaḷ mutaliyār kaṅkappiḷḷai Āṉa rā[ja*]rā[ja*]
kkaṅkai nāṭāḻvār nāyanāṟku Aṭimaiyāka kuṭuttamaiyil Ivarkaḷaiyum piṉpu kaḻani Āṇṭiyai yum peṟṟāṉaiyum Āṇṭāḷiyaiyum Āṇ
ṭāṉaiyum tirukkaḷammaḻakiyāṉaiyum Ivarkaḷaiyum Ivar(ṟka)kaḷ vaṟkkattāraiyum viṟṟuk kuṭuttu koḷvatāṉa kācu Āyiramu
m cipaṇṭārattu kaikkoṇṭu Ivarkaḷai viṟṟukkuṭutteṉ Ariyāṉ piccaṉāṉa Etirili coḻak kaṅkaināṭāḻvāṉeṉ Ippaṭikku Ivai
etirilicoḻak kaṅkai nāṭāḻvāṉeḻuttum coḻaṉ Āṉaikāttāṉ Āṉa coḻiya varaiyaṉum kūttaṉ tiruvekampa muṭaiyāṉ rājarājak kaṅkai nāṭāḻvāṉum
cayaññaiyātaṉamaikkum Ippaṭikku Ivai Ariyanāyakaveḷāṉ Eḻuttu U U
Digital edition of SII 17.541 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
